Name: Azumarill
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn, when you play Azumarill from your hand to evolve 1 of your Active Pokémon, you may use this power. Each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Effects: Flip a coin for each [W] Energy attached to Azumarill. This attack does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each heads.

Name: Dark Ampharos
Ability-Effect: As long as Dark Ampharos is in play, whenever your opponent plays an Evolution card from his or her hand to evolve 1 of his or her Pokémon, put 2 damage counters on that Pokémon. You can't use more than 1 Darkest Impulse Poké-Body each turn.
Effects: Discard all [L] Energy attached to Dark Ampharos.

Name: Dark Crobat
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dark Crobat is your Active Pokémon, you may choose 1 of the Defending Pokémon. That Pokémon is now Poisoned. This power can't be used if Dark Crobat is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 10 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. ) Remove from Dark Crobat a number of damage counters equal to the number of your opponent's Pokémon in play.
Does 30 damage to 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Dark Electrode
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Dark Electrode has no Energy attached to it, you may search your deck for a [D] or Dark [M] Energy and attach it to Dark Electrode. Shuffle your deck afterward. This power can't be used if Dark Electrode is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may move all Energy cards attached to Dark Electrode to your Benched Pokémon in any way you like.

Name: Dark Houndoom
Effects: Choose a card from your opponent's hand without looking and discard it.
You may discard a [R] Energy or [D] Energy attached to Dark Houndoom. If you do, this attack does 40 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Dark Hypno
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, choose an attack on 1 of your Pokémon in play that has Dark in its name (excluding this one). Dark Link copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack. ) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Dark Hypno's type is still [P] [D]. ) Dark Hypno performs that attack.
Does 20 damage times the number of your opponent's Benched Pokémon.

Name: Dark Marowak
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.
Discard a Basic Pokémon or Evolution card from your hand or this attack does nothing.

Name: Dark Octillery
Effects: Does 10 damage to each Defending Pokémon. Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon is now Paralyzed.
Does 30 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Octillery but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 20 damage in this way.

Name: Dark Slowking
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), you may look at the top card of your opponent's deck. Then, you may shuffle his or her deck. This power can't be used if Dark Slowking is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: You may discard a combination of up to 2 Pokémon Tool cards and Rocket's Secret Machine cards from your hand. If you do, this attack does 20 damage plus 30 more damage for each card you discarded.

Name: Dark Steelix
Effects: Search your discard pile for an Energy card and attach it to Dark Steelix.

Name: Jumpluff
Ability-Effect: If Jumpluff would be Knocked Out by an opponent's attack, flip a coin. If heads, Jumpluff is not Knocked Out and its remaining HP becomes 10 instead.
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to all of your opponent's Pokémon.

Name: Kingdra
Ability-Effect: As long as Kingdra is in play, each of your Active Pokémon has no Weakness.
Effects: Flip a coin until you get tails. For each heads, discard an Energy card attached to the Defending Pokémon.
This attack's damage is not affected by Resistance.

Name: Piloswine
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, each Defending Pokémon can't attack during your opponent's next turn.
You may do 60 damage plus 40 more damage. If you do, Piloswine does 30 damage to itself.

Ability-Effect: Prevent all effects of attacks, including damage, done to Togetic by your opponent's Pokémon that has Dark in its name.
Effects: Choose 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon's attacks. Rainbow Moves copies that attack except for its Energy cost. (You must still do anything else required for that attack. ) (No matter what type that Pokémon is, Togetic's type is still [C]. ) Togetic performs that attack.

Name: Dark Dragonite
Ability-Effect: As often as you like during your turn (before your attack), you may move a [D] Energy card attached to 1 of your Pokémon to another of your Pokémon. This power can't be used if Dark Dragonite is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Does 30 damage to each Defending Pokémon.

Name: Dark Muk
Ability-Effect: As long as Dark Muk is your Active Pokémon, your opponent pays [C][C] more to retreat his or her Active Pokémon.
Effects: Does 10 damage times the number of [C] Energy in the Defending Pokémon's Retreat Cost (after applying effects to the Retreat Cost).
The Defending Pokémon is now Burned and Poisoned.

Name: Dark Raticate
Effects: The Defending Pokémon can't retreat during your opponent's next turn. Put 5 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon at the end of your opponent's next turn.
The Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned. This attack does 20 damage to 1 of your opponent's Benched Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )

Name: Dark Sandslash
Ability-Effect: If Dark Sandslash is your Active Pokémon and is damaged by an opponent's attack (even if Dark Sandslash is Knocked Out), the Attacking Pokémon is now Poisoned.
Effects: This attack's damage isn't affected by Weakness, Resistance, Poké-Powers, Poké-Bodies, or any other effects on the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Dark Tyranitar
Effects: Does 10 damage plus 10 more damage for each Energy attached to Dark Tyranitar.
Does 20 damage to each of your opponent's Pokémon. (Don't apply Weakness and Resistance for Benched Pokémon. )
If the Defending Pokémon is Pokémon-ex, this attack does 70 damage plus 50 more damage.

Name: Dark Tyranitar
Ability-Effect: As long as Dark Tyranitar is your Active Pokémon, put 1 damage counter on each of your opponent's Benched Basic Pokémon between turns. You can't use more than 1 Sand Damage Poké-Body between turns.
Effects: If the Defending Pokémon already has at least 2 damage counters on it, this attack does 50 damage plus 20 more damage.

Name: Delibird
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Delibird is your Active Pokémon, you may shuffle 1 card from your hand into your deck. Then, draw a card. This power can't be used if Delibird is affected by a Special Condition.
Effects: Flip 3 coins. If 1 of them is heads, put 4 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If 2 of them are heads, remove 1 damage counter from the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are heads, put 10 damage counters on the Defending Pokémon. If all of them are tails, remove all damage counters from the Defending Pokémon.

Name: Furret
Effects: Choose up to 3 cards in your hand and put them on top of your deck. Then, search your deck for that many cards and put them into your hand. Shuffle your deck afterward.
Before doing damage, you may flip a coin. If heads, this attack does 80 damage instead. If tails, this attack does nothing.

Name: Ledian
Ability-Effect: Any damage done to Ledian by attacks from Pokémon that has an owner in its name is reduced by 40.
Effects: Flip a coin. If heads, the Defending Pokémon is now Confused.

Effects: If you have a Supporter card in play, use the effect on that card as the effect of this attack.

Name: Misdreavus
Ability-Effect: Once during your turn (before your attack), if Misdreavus is your Active Pokémon, you may flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. This power can't be used if Misdreavus is affected by a Special Condition or if your other Active Pokémon is not Misdreavus.
Effects: Switch Misdreavus with 1 of your Benched Pokémon.

Effects: Does 20 damage plus 20 more damage for each basic Energy card attached to Quagsire but not used to pay for this attack's Energy cost. You can't add more than 60 damage in this way.

Effects: Each Defending Pokémon is now Poisoned.

Name: Surprise! Time Machine

If that Pokémon remains in play, search your deck for an Evolution card that evolves from that Pokémon and put it onto that Pokémon (this counts as evolving that Pokémon). Shuffle your deck afterward.

(Any cards attached to that Pokémon, damage counters, Special Conditions, and effects on it are now on the new Pokémon. ) Place the first Basic Pokémon in the discard pile. Shuffle your deck afterward.

Description: Flip a coin. If heads, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your opponent's Pokémon. If tails, put 1 damage counter on 1 of your Pokémon.

Name: Dark Metal Energy

Name: R Energy
Description: R Energy can be attached only to a Pokémon that has Dark or Rocket's in its name. While in play, R Energy provides 2 [D] Energy. (Doesn't count as a basic Energy card. ) If the Pokémon R Energy is attached to attacks, the attack does 10 more damage to the Active Pokémon (before applying Weakness and Resistance). When your turn ends, discard R Energy.
